parsed.summary: Cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff touches 84 files (+6 623/−480 lines). The highest-risk changes—retry.mjs (203 new lines of backoff/retry), bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines of error classification), glm-client.mjs rewrite (155 lines changed), and background job lifecycle via tracked-jobs.mjs—are structurally plausible but cannot be adequately validated from diff-stat alone. Several concrete structural concerns survive even without full source access.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted—consumer import breakage risk
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was entirely removed and replaced by the new scripts/lib/retry.mjs (203 lines). If any consumer imports from './process' or './lib/process' via static or dynamic import, the call site will throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at runtime. The rename also invalidates any cached module-resolution metadata in running sessions, meaning an in-place upgrade without restart silently keeps using the old in-memory module until the process recycles. This is exactly the kind of failure that passes unit tests (which use fresh imports) but breaks live sessions.
findings.recommendation: Grepping the entire codebase for 'process\.mjs' and './process' to confirm zero remaining references. Add a re-export shim at scripts/lib/process.mjs that re-exports from retry.mjs with a deprecation warning for at least one minor version to hedge against external consumers or stale require caches.

findings.title: retry.mjs 203 lines of new backoff logic—no evidence of jitter or hard ceiling from structural analysis
findings.body: retry.mjs is the largest single new module. Exponential backoff without pseudorandom jitter creates a thundering-herd risk when multiple clients retry simultaneously against the same BigModel endpoint. A missing absolute retry ceiling (beyond just maxAttempts) can produce unbounded wall-clock latency on cascading transient failures. The companion tests/retry.test.mjs (352 lines) suggests coverage exists, but the test:code ratio alone does not confirm jitter or cap invariants were asserted—only source inspection can.
findings.recommendation: Verify in retry.mjs that: (1) each backoff delay includes random jitter of at least ±25%, (2) there is a hard wall-clock timeout independent of attempt count, (3) tests explicitly assert both properties. If any are missing, add them before ship.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs now wraps API calls with retry—non-idempotent request replay risk
findings.body: glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) integrates the new retry and error-dispatch layers. If BigModel API calls are not idempotent (e.g., job submission, state mutations), a retry on a network timeout that actually reached the server will duplicate the operation. The absence of an idempotency-key header or request-deduplication guard in the diff stat suggests this path may be unprotected.
findings.recommendation: Classify each API endpoint call in glm-client.mjs as idempotent (GET, HEAD) vs non-idempotent (POST, PUT with side effects). For non-idempotent calls, either (a) do not retry on timeout, or (b) attach an idempotency key header and confirm the server honors it. Add a test that verifies non-idempotent calls are never retried on ambiguous errors.

findings.title: Background job lifecycle (--wait/--background) introduces orphaned-process and state-drift surface
findings.body: v0.4.5 adds --background execution mode (job-control.mjs 20 lines changed, tracked-jobs.mjs 78 lines changed, render.mjs 79 lines changed). A backgrounded job whose parent process is killed (SIGKILL, OOM) cannot write its exit status to state, leaving tracked-jobs in a permanently 'running' state with no recovery path. The diff stat does not show a stale-job reap or TTL mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Add a stale-job reaper: on startup (or periodically), scan tracked-jobs for entries in 'running' state whose PID is no longer alive (kill(pid,0) === ESRCH), and mark them as 'lost' or 'crashed'. Expose this via /glm:status so users can discover orphaned runs. Consider writing a heartbeat timestamp so jobs stalled beyond a configurable TTL are also flagged.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification—misclassification silently swallows or double-retries
findings.body: The new 168-line error dispatch module classifies upstream errors into retriable vs permanent categories. A false-negative (marking a retriable error as permanent) causes silent failure with no retry; a false-positive (marking a permanent error as retriable) wastes quota and may rate-limit the account. Either misclassification is invisible to the user unless the classification logic is logged verbatim.
findings.recommendation: Ensure every classification decision in bigmodel-errors.mjs emits a structured log entry (error code, raw status, classification result). Add a tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs case for each known HTTP status code the BigModel API returns, including edge cases like 429 with Retry-After header, 502/503/504 gateway errors, and malformed response bodies.

findings.title: state.mjs 32-line diff—file-based state has no advisory lock or atomic write
findings.body: state.mjs persists runtime state to the filesystem. If two glm-companion processes run concurrently (e.g., two terminal tabs), both may read-modify-write the same state file without locking, producing interleaved writes that corrupt JSON. The diff stat does not show a lockfile or atomic-rename write pattern being added.
findings.recommendation: Use write-to-temp-then-rename (atomic on POSIX) for all state writes, or add an advisory lockfile (lockfile.mjs / proper-lockfile pattern). At minimum, wrap writes in a try/catch that validates the written file parses as valid JSON and retry if corrupted.

next_steps: Run: git grep -n 'process\.mjs\|/process' -- '*.mjs' '*.js' '*.cjs' to confirm zero remaining references to the deleted module.
Inspect retry.mjs for jitter, max-wall-time cap, and attempt-count ceiling. Verify tests assert all three.
Audit glm-client.mjs call sites for non-idempotent API calls that are wrapped in retry; confirm each is either safe to replay or excluded from retry.
Check tracked-jobs.mjs for any stale-job reaper, TTL, or heartbeat mechanism. If absent, this is a latent data-loss bug.
Verify bigmodel-errors.mjs logs every classification decision and that tests cover all documented BigModel HTTP error codes.
Run full test suite and confirm zero failures before merge.
